scenario: Apex Retail, a retailer attaching 12 boundary webhooks to in-flight order tasks at a peak of 9000 orders per hour on Camunda 8.8, ships a custom boundary event Webhook connector template. A template author needs to confirm which HTTP method choices the Webhook method dropdown lists by default and what value drives the default behaviour when the partner system does not specify a method.
question: Which choices does the inbound boundary event Webhook connector template list for the Webhook method dropdown, and which is the default?

options.text: Choices CONNECT, OPTIONS, GET, POST, with default OPTIONS so the connector replies to CORS preflight requests automatically before invoking the boundary correlation logic
Choices Any, GET, POST, PUT, DELETE, with default value any so the webhook accepts requests irrespective of the HTTP method used by the caller
Choices Any, GET, POST, PATCH, DELETE, with default value get because the boundary event template assumes idempotent reads and reserves writes for outbound connectors
Choices PUT and POST only, with default value post since HMAC signatures on boundary events are only documented for these two write-style methods in the template

a.text: Incorrect. The documented dropdown does not include CONNECT or OPTIONS, and the default value is any, not OPTIONS.

b.text: Correct. The boundary event template defines the Webhook method dropdown with choices Any, GET, POST, PUT, and DELETE, and the default value is any so the webhook accepts requests irrespective of the HTTP method used.

c.text: Incorrect. PATCH is not in the dropdown for the boundary event template, and the default is any rather than get.

d.text: Incorrect. The dropdown lists more than two methods (Any, GET, POST, PUT, DELETE) and HMAC support is not limited to PUT or POST in the template definition.

explanation: The boundary event Webhook connector template defines the Webhook method as a Dropdown bound to inbound.method with choices Any, GET, POST, PUT, and DELETE. The default value any lets the webhook accept requests regardless of HTTP method, leaving filtering to the activation condition.

docs.title: Connector templates - Inbound boundary event connector templates
